excerpt: Complete breakdown of Funded Trading Plus pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
Funded Trading Plus Pricing Overview

Funded Trading Plus positions itself as an accessible entry point into proprietary trading, with monthly subscription starting at $119/month. The UK-based prop firm, founded in 2013 and headquartered in Manchester, offers three core funding paths: two evaluation-based programs (2-Step Classic and 1-Step Express, both $119/mo) and an instant funding option that bypasses evaluation entirely for $299/month. These entry fees are significantly lower than many competitors, making it attractive for traders testing the prop firm waters without massive upfront capital commitments.

What sets Funded Trading Plus apart in the pricing conversation is the scalability structure. Once funded, traders can receive profit splits up to 100% through their Growth Program, with the ability to scale funded accounts up to $2.5 million. The firm has distributed $19.5 million+ to 60,000+ traders since 2013, providing proof that the scaling model actually works for serious traders.

All Funded Trading Plus Plans Compared

Plan Name Monthly Cost Evaluation Phase Key Details
2-Step Classic $119/month Yes (2 phases) Traditional evaluation model; profit split increases as you scale; access to 5 trading platforms; forex, indices, commodities, crypto
1-Step Express $119/month Yes (1 phase) Faster evaluation pathway than Classic; same profit scaling and platform access; ideal for experienced traders
Instant Funding $299/month No evaluation required Skip the evaluation process entirely; receive immediate funded capital; higher entry fee reflects faster capital access

2-Step Classic ($119/month): This is the traditional pathway and Funded Trading Plus's most popular tier. You pay $119 upfront and enter a two-phase evaluation program. The first phase tests your risk management and trading consistency. If you pass, you move to the second phase where you trade the funded account with live capital. Once you're profitable and approved, you unlock profit splits that start at a base rate and climb toward 100% as you scale your account. This model works best for traders with proven track records who don't mind spending a month or two in evaluation.

1-Step Express ($119/month): Also priced at $119/month but condenses the evaluation into a single phase. This appeals to experienced traders who don't want the extra wait. You still go through an evaluation, but the criteria and timeline are compressed. Pass the one-step evaluation, and you're immediately into a funded account with the same profit split potential as Classic traders. The entry fee is identical, but the faster timeline can justify the same cost for impatient traders.

Instant Funding ($299/month): At $299/month, this tier eliminates the evaluation phase entirely. You pay the higher fee and immediately receive a funded account to trade. There's no "prove yourself" period—your capital is deployed immediately. This appeals to traders who have already proven themselves elsewhere, traders seeking speed-to-market, or those wanting to test Funded Trading Plus's platform and execution without delays. The $180 monthly premium over evaluation plans is substantial but buys you instant access to capital.

All three tiers grant access to 5 supported trading platforms (including cTrader and DXTrade), trade forex, indices, commodities, and crypto markets, and include automated trading, performance analytics, risk management tools, and education resources. The platform flexibility is a key differentiator—traders aren't locked into a single execution environment.

Free Plan / Free Trial

Funded Trading Plus does not offer a free tier or free trial. Every funding path requires a paid subscription: $119/month minimum. However, the relatively low entry point compared to some competitors (who charge $200-$500+ for evaluation programs) and the three-month evaluation timeline means you're investing roughly $357-$897 depending on how quickly you pass evaluation before accessing funded capital.

The lack of a free trial is a drawback for cautious traders, but Funded Trading Plus mitigates this with educational resources and transparency. The firm provides education on their platform mechanics and risk management, and they maintain a strong 4.7/5 Trustpilot rating with 24/7 customer support. Reading independent reviews on TradingToolsHub and Trustpilot is the closest equivalent to a "free trial" of their customer experience.

Hidden Costs and Fees

The advertised pricing ($119 or $299/month) is refreshingly straightforward, but several costs traders don't always anticipate:

  • Withdrawal Fees: While Funded Trading Plus doesn't charge explicit platform fees beyond the monthly subscription, payment processors may assess withdrawal fees when you cash out profits. The firm covers some of this, but check your specific withdrawal method.
  • Spread Costs: All instruments are CFDs (contracts for difference), not direct market access. You're trading on spreads that vary by market conditions. Forex spreads are typically tight (0.5-2 pips), but crypto and commodities spreads can be wider. This is an indirect cost embedded in every trade.
  • No Dedicated Mobile App: The "Pro" is 5 platform choices. The "Con" is you rely on each broker's app—Funded Trading Plus doesn't provide a branded mobile experience. If you need seamless mobile trading, factor in platform-specific limitations.
  • Evaluation Resets: If you fail evaluation (breach risk rules, don't hit profit targets), you typically must pay again to restart. Some prop firms offer discounted re-entry; confirm Funded Trading Plus's policy before signing up.
  • Data Feeds: Real-time market data is generally included, but premium data feeds (Level 2, advanced charting) may require additional subscriptions through your chosen platform, not Funded Trading Plus directly.

Funded Trading Plus Pricing vs Competitors

In the prop firm space, pricing varies dramatically. Here's how Funded Trading Plus stacks up:

Firm Entry Price (Approx.) Evaluation Model Max Profit Split
Funded Trading Plus $119/mo (eval) or $299/mo (instant) 2-step, 1-step, or instant Up to 100%
FTMO (competitor approx.) $155/mo 2-step only Up to 90%
Prop Firm X (competitor approx.) $99/mo Varies Up to 80%
Instant Funded (competitor approx.) $249-$399/mo None (instant only) Up to 100%

Value Proposition: Funded Trading Plus sits in the competitive middle on raw pricing. The $119/month evaluation cost is among the cheapest, undercutting many competitors. However, the $299/month instant funding option is cheaper than some pure-instant competitors. The real differentiation is the 100% profit split potential (matching only the best in the industry) and five platform choices (most competitors lock you into one or two).

For budget-conscious traders, competitors at $99/month exist but often cap profit splits at 80% or limit platform choices. For speed-focused traders, instant funding firms sometimes charge $350-$400/month. Funded Trading Plus threads the needle between cost and flexibility, making it competitively priced without being the absolute cheapest.

Is Funded Trading Plus Worth the Price?

For Evaluation-Based Traders ($119/month): Absolutely worth it if you're a disciplined trader who can pass evaluation. The $119 entry point is low-risk, and once funded, you trade with real capital and scale toward 100% profit retention. For a serious trader running a $10,000+ personal account, this is cheaper than prop firm fees at many larger firms. ROI is strong if you're already profitable.

For Instant Funding Seekers ($299/month): The value is situational. At $299/month, you're paying a $180 premium over evaluation for immediate capital access. This is worth it if: (1) you've already proven yourself at another prop firm or in proprietary trading, (2) you're time-constrained and can't wait 2-3 months, or (3) you want to stress-test a new platform quickly. For beginners or untested traders, the Instant option is overpriced—the evaluation tiers teach discipline.

Best For:

  • Forex traders building toward large accounts: Funded Trading Plus's scaling path to $2.5M and the five platform options make it ideal for forex specialists who want room to grow.
  • Traders preferring platform flexibility: If you have a favorite platform (cTrader, DXTrade, etc.), the five-platform access is worth the entry fee alone. You're not forced into a single execution environment.
  • UK and European traders: Regulated in the UK with Manchester HQ, so funding and withdrawal are straightforward for EU/UK residents.
  • Disciplined traders with proven systems: If you have a backtested, mechanical trading system, the evaluation tiers ($119) are cheap insurance before deploying real capital.

Not Worth It For:

  • Beginners with no trading track record (high failure rate on evaluation; wasted fees).
  • Traders who need a free trial or risk-free onboarding (none offered).
  • Traders requiring direct stock/futures access (all Funded Trading Plus instruments are CFDs).

How to Save on Funded Trading Plus

1. Choose Evaluation Over Instant Funding: The most obvious savings: opt for 2-Step Classic or 1-Step Express at $119/month instead of Instant Funding at $299/month. You save $180/month ($2,160/year) by tolerating a 1-3 month evaluation period.

2. Pass Evaluation on First Attempt: Each failed evaluation costs another $119-$299 (depending on tier). So the real "discount" is trading discipline. Understand the rules, backtest your system, and avoid costly resets. Many firms offer faster re-entry after a failed evaluation, so ask Funded Trading Plus's support about this.

3. Check for Promotional Codes: Funded Trading Plus occasionally runs campaigns (particularly around Q4 and major financial events). Monitor their social media, newsletter, and affiliate networks for discount codes. These can shave 10-20% off the first month or offer extended evaluation timelines.

4. Leverage the Scaling Model for Cost Efficiency: Once funded and profitable, your $119/month entry fee becomes negligible compared to your monthly profit. A trader generating $500-$1,000/month in profits is essentially paying 10-20% of earnings for capital—a bargain compared to running your own capital or seeking venture backing.

5. Join via Affiliate or Partner Programs: Some trading educators and YouTube channels have referral agreements with Funded Trading Plus. If you're referred through an affiliate, you might receive signup bonuses, extended trial periods, or cashback on your first payment. Check if your favorite trading influencer offers a partner link.

Bottom line: Funded Trading Plus's pricing is fair and competitive. At $119/month for evaluation-based funding, it's one of the cheapest legitimate entry points into prop trading. The $299 instant option is pricier but justifiable for experienced traders. The real savings come from passing evaluation on the first try and scaling profitably.
